info.title: Agent API
info.description: Hyperweaver Agent API v1 — the shared host-agent contract (architecture D1), as implemented by hyperweaver-agent (VirtualBox/Vagrant, Go). Reference implementation: zoneweaver-agent (Bhyve/OmniOS, Node). Capabilities are advertised by the public GET /api/status (role, hypervisors, console, auth, features) and drive conditional UI rendering. This document describes only the endpoints this agent implements today and grows with it; machine creation (POST /machines) and the console group land with the provisioning engine. The implementing application version is `info.x-app-version`; `info.version` is the frozen contract line. Authorization is role-based (admin > operator > viewer) and enforced by a central method+path policy; each operation notes its minimum role.

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Public identity and capability discovery (Hyperweaver dual-mode contract)

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: API-key lifecycle (Agent API v1 local auth tier)

tags.name: Local Login
tags.description: Local-presence sign-in: the tray Open handoff and the hwa:// protocol handler

tags.name: Task Management
tags.description: The task queue: every machine operation runs as a prioritized, dependency-chained task with streamed output

tags.name: Machine Management
tags.description: VirtualBox machines — vagrant-backed or raw — discovered by reconciliation and operated through queued tasks

tags.name: Swap Management
tags.description: Read-only swap information (the swap capability token); add/remove are OmniOS semantics and not implemented on this agent

tags.name: Settings
tags.description: Agent configuration over the API (admin only)

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key in Bearer format: `Authorization: Bearer hw_your_api_key_here`

XApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
XApiKeyAuth.description: API key in the X-API-Key header (equivalent to the Bearer form)

Status.description: Public identity + capabilities document (drives dual-mode UI rendering)

description.example: API key for the Hyperweaver Server

role.description: Authorization role of the key (Agent API v1 role model)

Tool.description: One detected prerequisite (SHI parity: presence + version)

Task.description: One task-queue entry (Agent API v1 Task schema).

operation.description: Operation kind; the executable vocabulary grows with the machines/provisioning phases

status.description: completed_with_errors is a parent-task terminal state: some children failed, some completed

priority.description: Higher runs first: 100 critical, 80 high, 60 medium, 50 service, 40 low, 20 background

created_by.description: API key name (or system source) that created the task

depends_on.description: Predecessor that must complete first; its failure or cancellation cancels this task

parent_task_id.description: Progress-aggregation anchor this task reports into

updatedAt.description: Last modification — the since query parameter compares against this

progress_info.description: Freeform progress detail; parent tasks carry {completed_tasks, failed_tasks, total_tasks, status}

output.description: JSON-encoded array of output entries as last flushed; GET /tasks/{taskId}/output serves the parsed, live view

Machine.description: One machine registry row (Agent API v1 Machine schema; backing/home are this agent's dual-path fields). Machine names are free user-chosen identifiers (design D-G).

status.description: VirtualBox is authoritative; configured means a registry row with no VM behind it yet

backing.description: Provenance metadata: vagrant means a vagrant project (home) owns this VM — used by provisioning operations. Lifecycle always drives VBoxManage directly.

server_id.description: Numeric server identifier (assigned at creation; see /machines/ids)

is_orphaned.description: In the registry but no longer present in VirtualBox

auto_discovered.description: Imported by the reconciliation sweep rather than created through the agent

configuration.description: Live machine configuration (VBoxManage showvminfo --machinereadable key/value map on this agent)

ServerStats.description: Host statistics in Node's os-module vocabulary (the shared v1 stats shape) plus the machine name lists.

cpus.description: One entry per logical CPU, Node os.cpus() shape; times are cumulative milliseconds — consumers diff between polls for usage

uptime.description: Host uptime in seconds (not process uptime)

allmachines.description: All registered machine names (VBoxManage list vms)

get.description: Canonical path of the public status probe. No authentication.

get.summary: Public identity and capabilities (SPA discovery alias)
get.description: Unconditional alias of GET /status — the single mode-discovery probe URL for the Hyperweaver UI. No authentication.

Minimum role: viewer — unless the agent is configured with `stats.public_access: true`, which serves this endpoint without an API key (the Node agent's conditional /stats registration). OS-level host statistics plus the registered and running VirtualBox machine name lists. Machine-list failures degrade to empty arrays — a broken VBoxManage never fails the host stats.

post.description: Public, but gated: requires `api_keys.bootstrap_enabled`, locks after the first key when `bootstrap_auto_disable` is on, and (by default) demands the setup claim token written to `setup.token` beside the agent's config file and printed to the startup log — proof of host ownership. The first key is always an admin key.

setupToken.description: 64-character claim token from the agent host

200.description: Key created — shown once, only a hash is stored

note.example: Bootstrap endpoint will be auto-disabled for future requests

403.description: Bootstrap disabled, auto-disabled, or setup token invalid

post.summary: Exchange a tray one-time token for an admin API key
post.description: Public: the token itself is the credential — minted seconds earlier by the local user's physical tray Open click (or an hwa:// protocol invocation), carried in the URL fragment, single-use, 60-second TTL. This is how a desktop user gets a signed-in UI without ever seeing a login screen.

token.description: Single-use tray token from the #tray= URL fragment

200.description: Fresh admin key, named after the local OS account

post.description: Public but secret-gated: when the OS spawns a fresh agent process for an hwa://open invocation (Windows registry handler, Linux .desktop handler), that process forwards the action here and exits. The per-boot secret file (0600, beside the running agent's config) authenticates it — web pages cannot read local files, so possession proves a local same-user process. On success the running agent opens the signed-in UI in the user's browser, exactly like a tray Open click.

secret.description: Contents of the running agent's protocol.secret file

200.description: Action accepted; the agent is opening the browser

description.example: API key for the Hyperweaver Server

200.description: Key created — shown once, only a hash is stored

get.description: Minimum role: admin. Hashes are never returned.
Minimum role: viewer (every valid key may inspect itself).

delete.description: Minimum role: admin. Deleting the last active admin key is refused (lockout guard).

409.description: Would remove the last active admin key

put.description: Minimum role: admin. Revoking the last active admin key is refused (lockout guard).

409.description: Would deactivate the last active admin key

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Includes the detected provisioning prerequisites (Vagrant, VirtualBox, Git) and host information — SHI's footer data.
Minimum role: viewer. Fetches the configured versioninfo document (updates.versioninfo_url) and compares against the running build.
Minimum role: viewer. A bare tool-name → installed map; this agent's tools are vagrant, virtualbox, and git (the Node agent lists its OmniOS set — same endpoint, platform-appropriate contents).
Minimum role: viewer. Filterable and sortable; the total count is computed only when include_count=true.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Only tasks modified (updatedAt) at or after this time — incremental refresh

parameters.name: include_count
parameters.description: Also return the total matching count

get.description: Minimum role: viewer.
Minimum role: viewer.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Pending tasks are cancelled immediately. Running tasks are cancellable too (this agent's D-F extension over the Node reference): the executor's child processes are killed, its per-operation cleanup runs, and the task lands in cancelled with its output preserved. Tasks pending on a cancelled task are cancelled by dependency propagation.

400.description: Task already in a terminal state

error.example: Can only cancel pending or running tasks

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. The live in-memory buffer while the task runs; the persisted output afterwards.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Hard-deletes every completed, completed_with_errors, failed, and cancelled task immediately. Pending and running tasks are untouched.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Machines built outside the agent appear here once the reconciliation sweep imports them.
Minimum role: viewer. Used server_ids, constraints, and the next available id (auto-assigned MAX+1, design D-G).
Minimum role: viewer.
Minimum role: viewer. Live-checks VirtualBox (updating the registry on drift) and returns the record, its live configuration, and pending tasks.

delete.description: Minimum role: operator. Running machines need force=true, which chains a CRITICAL stop before the CRITICAL delete. The machine is powered off if still running, unregistered from VirtualBox with media deletion, its registry row removed, and its leftover pending tasks cancelled.

400.description: Machine is running and force is not set

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. The live configuration document (VBoxManage showvminfo --machinereadable view on this agent), falling back to the last reconciled copy.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a start task: VBoxManage startvm --type headless — immediate power-on, no provisioning machinery. Idempotent: already-running machines answer already_running; an existing pending/running start task is reused.

200.description: Start task queued (or machine already running)

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a stop task and cancels pending starts: ACPI power button (graceful), falling back to hard poweroff when the guest ignores it — or straight poweroff with force=true.

200.description: Stop task queued (or machine already stopped)

post.description: Minimum role: operator. Queues a HIGH-priority stop and a MEDIUM-priority start chained on it.
Minimum role: operator. Saves the machine's state (VBoxManage controlvm savestate). Advertised by the machine-suspend capability token — the UI shows Suspend only on agents that carry it.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer.

put.description: Minimum role: operator. Registry-only, no task.

get.description: Minimum role: viewer.

put.description: Minimum role: operator. Registry-only, no task.

post.description: Minimum role: operator. machines is "all" (every stopped, non-orphaned machine) or a name array; already-running machines are skipped with a reason.

machines.description: "all" or an array of machine names

post.description: Minimum role: operator. machines is "all" (every running, non-orphaned machine) or a name array; already-stopped machines are skipped with a reason. Pending starts for targeted machines are cancelled.

machines.description: "all" or an array of machine names

get.description: Minimum role: viewer. Aggregate swap figures, per-area breakdown, and utilization recommendations (Node-agent shape). Pool fields are present but empty — ZFS pools have no analog on this host. Read live from the OS; lastScanned is the request time.
Minimum role: viewer. Row-per-area listing with the Node payload's pagination envelope, read live from the OS. The zvol pool filter has no meaning on this agent and is ignored.
Minimum role: admin. Returns the live configuration with the same section and key names as config.yaml.

put.description: Minimum role: admin. Shallow-merges the submitted top-level sections onto the on-disk YAML, validates the result, backs up the current file, and writes atomically. The running process keeps its loaded values — most changes apply on restart.

message.example: Settings updated successfully. Some changes may require a server restart.

500.description: Merged configuration invalid or write failure

get.description: Minimum role: admin. Section/field types, descriptions, defaults, ranges, and restart requirements — the document the settings UI renders its tabs from.
Minimum role: admin. Newest first.

delete.description: Minimum role: admin. Only agent-generated backup names (config-<unix-ms>.yaml) are accepted.

post.description: Minimum role: admin. The backup is validated as a working configuration and the current file is backed up first.
Minimum role: admin. Under systemd the unit's Restart=always brings the agent back after a clean exit; everywhere else the agent spawns its own successor before shutting down.

message.example: Server restart initiated. Please wait a few seconds before reconnecting. The server will reload all configuration changes.
